title: Sodium thiosulfate-free silver staining for PAGE
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"lsnk","text":"This protocol describes a silver staining method for visualizing proteins separated by SDS-PAGE without using sodium thiosulfate as a sensitizer. The procedure involves fixation of the gel to immobilize proteins and reduce background, followed by impregnation with a silver nitrate solution that binds to the protein bands. After rinsing off excess silver, the gel is developed using a sodium carbonate and formaldehyde solution, which reduces silver ions to metallic silver at protein locations, creating visible bands. The reaction is stopped with a Tris-acetic acid solution to stabilize the image and prevent overdevelopment. Final washes remove residual chemicals, resulting in sensitive protein detection with minimal background. This thiosulfate-free protocol is recommended when downstream applications like mass spectrometry require avoidance of certain chemical interferences.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"fxlat","text":"Handle solutions with care: Prepare silver nitrate and developing solutions fresh or store properly in dark containers to prevent degradation.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":27}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"foa56","text":"Protect from light: Perform silver impregnation and development steps in low light or darkness to avoid nonspecific silver reduction and background staining.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":19}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"31bru","text":"Optimize fixation: Ensure thorough fixation of the gel to reduce background and enhance protein band clarity.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":18}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"89mfe","text":"Wash thoroughly: Adequate washing between steps is critical to remove excess reagents and minimize background staining.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":16}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6v11n","text":"Monitor development time: Observe the gel carefully during development; stop the reaction promptly when bands are visible to prevent overdevelopment and high background.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":25}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1b413","text":"Be aware of reduced sensitivity: Omitting sodium thiosulfate may reduce staining sensitivity and contrast; adjust staining times accordingly.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":32}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"31sie","text":"Use appropriate downstream processing: This protocol is particularly useful when avoiding thiosulfate is necessary, such as prior to mass spectrometry analysis.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":38}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"d76hb","text":"Dispose of waste properly: Silver-containing solutions are hazardous; follow institutional guidelines for disposal.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":26}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"c5v9l","text":"Omission of Thiosulfate Sensitization: This protocol does not include a sensitization step with sodium thiosulfate to avoid chemical interferences that can affect downstream analyses such as mass spectrometry. Thiosulfate can form complexes with silver ions, which may cause protein modifications or artifacts during staining.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":38}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"chj39","text":"Reduced Sensitivity and Contrast: Without sodium thiosulfate, the sensitivity of protein detection may be lower, and background staining can be higher. Careful optimization of fixation, washing, and development times is critical to obtain clear and specific protein bands.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":33}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6c09g","text":"Handling Silver Solutions: Silver nitrate and related reagents are light-sensitive and should be protected from light to prevent nonspecific silver reduction. Silver-containing waste is hazardous; dispose of it according to institutional regulations and environmental safety guidelines.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":27}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"c020p","text":"Use of Chemicals: Formaldehyde used in development is toxic and volatile; handle it in a fume hood using appropriate personal protective equipment (PPE). Acetic acid and ethanol can irritate the skin and eyes; wear gloves and eye protection.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":18}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"eoft1","text":"Monitoring Development Time: Overdevelopment can lead to dark background and mask protein bands. Development should be stopped promptly once clear bands appear.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":28}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8rj9f","text":"Gel Handling: Avoid contamination of gels with metallic objects or dirty containers, which can cause precipitation of silver and artifacts.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":13}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4nhic","text":"Temperature Control: Staining reactions are temperature sensitive. Perform silver impregnation and development at recommended conditions to ensure reproducibility.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":20}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

Nitrate Solution (0.1-0.2%)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":34}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"v9otx","text":"- Silver nitrate (AgNO3): 1-2 g","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"viwyi","text":"- Ultrapure water: 1 L","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"rdqxd","text":"Prepare carefully and store in a dark bottle protected from Solution","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":19}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"nyshu","text":"- Sodium carbonate (Na2CO3) 2% (w/v): 20 g dissolved in 1 L ultrapure water","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"qbtpn","text":"- Formaldehyde (37%): Add 0.5 mL per 100 mL of sodium carbonate solution (final 0.05%)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1ffew","text":"Prepare fresh before use.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"nfqai","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"zwwzk","text":"Stopping Solution","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":17}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"uepkx","text":"- Tris base 50 mM: Dissolve 6.06 g in 1 L ultrapure water","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"vfptv","text":"- Acetic acid 10 mM: Add 0.6 mL glacial acetic acid per 1 L Tris solution","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6afm0","text":"Adjust pH to ~7.0","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Immerse gel in Fixing Solution for 00:30:00 at Room temperature with gentle agitation.
(Optional) It can be also fixed for 01:00:00 at Room temperature or Overnight at 4 °C to reduce background.
Wash gel 3 times for 00:10:00 each in ultrapure water with gentle agitation.
Incubate gel in Silver Nitrate Solution 0.1-0.2 Mass Percent for 00:30:00 at Room temperature in the dark or low light.
Rinse gel twice in ultrapure water for 00:00:30 to remove excess silver nitrate.
Immerse gel in Developing Solution and monitor band development for a maximum of 00:10:00 .
Stop development when bands are visible and before background darkens.
Transfer gel to Stopping Solution for 00:05:00 to stabilize staining.
Wash gel in ultrapure water for at least 00:15:00 to remove residual chemicals.
Store gel in ultrapure water or document bands by photographing.
creator: Agustín Sanchez-Temprano
